@judyjoseph
We found an issue with latest bmcctl that CLI "config chassis modules <shutdown/startup> SWITCH-HOST" events are not captured by Eventhandler untill PMON is restarted once after fresh onie-nos-install. 

Also, in air-cooled systems, if switch-host is shutdown and saved in DB, this commit skips power-on the switch-host on startup. 

Could you please review the changes?

@Junchao-Mellanox Please review this PR, with this change, `show platform temperature` will no longer display the SFPs

> @vvolam removing transceiver temperatures from `show platform temperature` is a big change. Is there an HLD for all the recent changes in this area and was there a community discussion? If yes, please can you share the recording link or date? I might have missed it. Thanks!

@aditya-nexthop The approach was to remove the transceiver temperature polling from thermalctld and use data polled by xcvrd. Xcvrd does this in two ways already 
1. dom manager thread, storing it in TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR_TABLE
2. new thread (introduced by PR : https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/pull/685), storing it in TRANSCEIVER_DOM_TEMPERATURE_TABLE

We had this comment earlier : https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/blob/e5da370eab1a1d424b8d6e02aa7353ad3ff0e6da/sonic-thermalctld/scripts/thermalctld#L866

So does nexthop platforms show sfp module temperature in "show plat temp" ?

adding @Junchao-Mellanox too.

> > @vvolam removing transceiver temperatures from `show platform temperature` is a big change. Is there an HLD for all the recent changes in this area and was there a community discussion? If yes, please can you share the recording link or date? I might have missed it. Thanks!
> 
> @aditya-nexthop The approach was to remove the transceiver temperature polling from thermalctld and use data polled by xcvrd. Xcvrd does this in two ways already
> 
> 1. dom manager thread, storing it in TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR_TABLE
> 2. new thread (introduced by PR : [[xcvrd] Add new thread to poll temperatures more aggressively #685](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/pull/685)), storing it in TRANSCEIVER_DOM_TEMPERATURE_TABLE
> 
> We had this comment earlier :
> 
> https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/blob/e5da370eab1a1d424b8d6e02aa7353ad3ff0e6da/sonic-thermalctld/scripts/thermalctld#L866

I think that getting the SFP temperature inline in thermalctld ensures that the values are fresh.
Going through the database assumes that xcvrd is able to poll temperatures at a rate matching or faster than thermalctld consumes them.

For platforms that have moved to using [[xcvrd] Add new thread to poll temperatures more aggressively #685](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/pull/685), this should be fine, but there might be other platforms that don't. Empirically, transceiver DOM (including temperature) updates at a frequency of 1 minute or slower (https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/pull/758) when polling via the DOM manager thread.
This might result in a change of behavior on platforms that poll transceiver temperature directly from thermalctld.

CC @prgeor @mihirpat1 
> 
> So does nexthop platforms show sfp module temperature in "show plat temp" ?

Yes our platforms currently do.
> 
> adding @Junchao-Mellanox too.

@aditya-nexthop just to bring context back again -- Based on the usecase and our internal lab testing where there could be a single device with like 500+ interfaces and sfp module temperature pooling loop in thermalctld affects its performance and application of cooling algorithm -- the approach is to move SFP module temperature polling out of thermalctld and let it be updated in xcvrd which anyways fetch this temperature info.

Thanks @judyjoseph for the context.

> SFP/transceiver temperature was being read from Redis (TRANSCEIVER_DOM_* tables populated by xcvrd) inside thermalctld's update loop. On high-port-count platforms (e.g. 512 SFPs), this caused 3-5 Redis round-trips per SFP per cycle, which could delay fast-polling sensors (such as ASIC temperature) and added duplicate ownership of transceiver telemetry between xcvrd and thermalctld.

But now the platform SFP objects will make the redis calls to get the SFP temperature from the TRANSCEIVER_DOM_* tables, right?
```
temperature = try_get(thermal.get_temperature)
```
Just having some trouble understanding how this results in a speedup.

Also thanks @vvolam for raising the PR to retain the SFP temperature values in `show platform temperature`

Both test_DaemonPcied.py and test_pcied.py call load_source("pcied", ...) which creates different module objects each time. When pytest imports both files, the second load_source call replaces sys.modules["pcied"] with a NEW module object. Then mock.patch("pcied.load_platform_pcieutil", ...) patches the NEW module, while DaemonPcied.__init__.__globals__ points to the OLD module's dict. So the mock doesn't affect the actual function that DaemonPcied.__init__ calls, causing it to run the real load_platform_pcieutil which returns None in the test environment (no platform paths available).

The fix: prevent double-loading by using conftest so we load the module only once across the tests for one daemon
We need to do this at least for all daemons where we have multiple test files.

Thanks @judyjoseph for the review and the good callout!

You're right that on high-port-count platforms (e.g. 512 SFPs), the SFP Redis reads could add up and potentially delay the next fast-polling cycle. Today each SFP costs 3-5 Redis round-trips per cycle, so at scale that's non-trivial.

That said, this concern exists independent of this PR — the SFP polling already runs in the same thread at 60s today. The per-component interval feature doesn't make it worse; it just makes the impact more visible when you have a fast-polling sensor like ASIC at 5s.

I agree the right approach is to remove SFP temperature polling from thermalctld entirely and let xcvrd own it (there's already a TODO in the code for this). I'd prefer to handle that as a separate PR since it's a different scope of change.

Would it be okay to merge this as-is and I'll file a follow-up to remove the SFP polling path?

https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-platform-daemons/blob/e5da370eab1a1d424b8d6e02aa7353ad3ff0e6da/sonic-chassisd/scripts/chassisd#L400

@bmridul

What this PR changes: When a daemon exits, it must delete only the entires it added to the `PHYSICAL_ENTITY_INFO` table in `STATE_DB`. Previously, the exiting daemons were deleting _all_ the entries in the `PHYSICAL_ENTITY_INFO` table, wiping out entries other daemons had written and breaking those daemons when they tried to read their own entries.

What this PR is not: It doesn't propose that daemons only delete entries at termination. That approach would leave `STATE_DB` full of stale entries during normal operation.

`Chassisd` writes to PHYSICAL_ENTITY_INFO as modules are added or removed. On termination, `Chassisd` already deletes only its own module entries, leaving the rest of the table intact.
